situation: audit-2026-07-08-046 derived fix SP-SCR-M-001: mail_push.py line 90 `int(os.environ.get('SMTP_PORT', '465'))` crashes with ValueError when SMTP_PORT env var is empty string or non-numeric. Cron environments may set SMTP_PORT='' explicitly, bypassing the default '465' fallback and causing unhandled exception that aborts the entire mail push.
action: Replace bare int() cast with try/except guard: strip whitespace from env value, fall back to 465 on empty or ValueError, emit warning to stderr for visibility. Single-file minimal route-fix per audit finding remediation.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh committed eb5c0d7 to auto/opt-scripts-1783396445, pushed to origin. Fix adds ValueError guard with stderr warning and default fallback.
next_suggestion: Continue dispatching remaining 8 pending route-fixes from audit-2026-07-08-046 (SP-SCR-M-003 social-scraper userAgent next)
After all pending fixes merged/rejected, cycle-complete triggers next full audit

audit_findings.finding: int(os.environ.get('SMTP_PORT', '465')) raises ValueError on empty string or non-numeric env value, crashing mail push silently in cron
audit_findings.remediation: Strip + try/except with default 465 fallback and stderr warning
